marketState: neutral, mild bullish tilt — extreme heat-driven demand and stretched short positioning offset by a comfortable storage surplus
summary: Front price ($2.911) is sitting almost exactly on the model's fair value ($2.923, spread -$0.012, status FAIR) at every horizon out to 21 days, so there is no valuation edge on the spot itself. The strongest signal in the data is weather: 8-16 day demand is +30.5% vs the 5-year norm and actually exceeds the historical max range, while 1-7 day demand is a more modest +9.1%. That sits alongside a managed-money COT position at just the 8th percentile of the last 5 years on net shorts (mmNet -105,709), raising short-covering risk if the hot weather verifies. Working against that is a clearly bearish storage picture (+180 Bcf vs 5y, and this week's build beat forecast by 22 Bcf), and weak, low-reliability seasonality (price already -7.7pp below its typical-year path, with only 38-44% historical odds of being higher in 1/3/4 weeks). The futures curve is roughly flat to slightly backwardated through Oct (front $2.911, Sep -$0.035, Oct -$0.006) before building into a steep contango toward the Dec/Jan winter strip ($3.735/$4.16), a normal seasonal shape that neither confirms nor contradicts a near-term move, while technicals split by timeframe (5m/15m constructive, 4h/1d/1w bearish with EMA50<200).

longTerm.entry: $2.80 - $2.90 (scale in on pullbacks toward current price/fair value)

longTerm.rationale: The fair-value curve itself is rising over the next three weeks ($2.923 -> $2.941 -> $2.985 -> $3.049), and 8-16 day demand is running above its historical max range (+30.5% vs 5y) — both point to firming physical tightness into peak summer cooling demand (seasonally strongest month is September). Managed money is positioned near a 5-year-low short percentile (8th), which adds asymmetric short-covering upside if the heat verifies. The counterweight is a +180 Bcf storage surplus vs 5y with a bearish build surprise and weak/low-reliability seasonality, which is why conviction is medium rather than high.
longTerm.invalidation: 8-16D weather demand forecast rolls over back toward/below the 5y norm, storage surprises keep printing bearish (further widening the +180 Bcf surplus), or price closes below ~$2.65 confirming the 1d/1w bearish technical trend is in control.

shortTerm.rationale: Front price is trading essentially AT fair value (spread only -$0.012, status FAIR) so there is no near-term mispricing edge, and technicals disagree by timeframe — 5m/15m lean neutral-to-bullish (RSI 52, EMA50>200) while 4h/1d/1w are bearish (EMA50<200, RSI 37-49). Near-term (1-7D) weather demand is only moderately elevated (+9.1% vs 5y, rangePos 0.63), not the extreme readout seen further out, and the front-to-Oct curve is flat/slightly backwardated with no strong term-structure signal, so it doesn't justify forcing a trade against mixed technicals.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either: (a) price breaking below ~$2.70-2.75 with 4h/1d/1w trend staying aligned bearish, confirming downside momentum for a short, or (b) 1h/4h technicals turning consistently bullish alongside a push through ~$3.00, confirming the weather-driven long thesis is being realized near-term.

Managed-money net short near 5-year-low percentile (8th) — short-covering risk if heat verifies
